---
title: "Workstation setup"
transition_status: "updated"
transition_comment: "update"
---

::: {.callout-tip title="Scope: Local machine (how)"}
  
This section focuses on setting up and maintaining your **local workstation** (software, configuration, automation via Ansible).
  
It answers: *How do I build and reproduce my machine?*
 
- For shared tools and platforms (e.g., communication, storage, scheduling tools), see → [Cloud services](cloud-services.html)
:::

## Resources

We maintain a reproducible workstation configuration based on Ansible.

- [{{< iconify fluent:code-24-regular >}}  Source workstation-setup repository (automation)](https://github.com/fs-ise/workstation-setup){.btn .btn-outline-primary rel="noopener" target=_blank}

- [{{< iconify fluent:document-24-regular >}} Documentation (how to use it)](https://github.com/fs-ise/workstation-setup/tree/main/docs){.btn .btn-outline-primary rel="noopener" target=_blank}

## Workstation stack

- **Base** (Minimal Fedora, debloated GNOME)
- **Dev** (Visual Studio Code, Git + LFS, Python, Docker, SSH)
- **Research** (Quarto, LaTeX, Pandoc tooling, Obsidian)
- **Documents** (Okular, OCR, GROBID, Recoll)
- **Sync & Backup** (Nextcloud, Vorta/Borg)
- **Productivity & Media** (Thunderbird, Chrome, GIMP, Audacity, OBS Studio)
- **Security** (KeePassXC, ClamAV)
